sourcePage.rawText:
15   The numbers a, b and c are each greater than 1.

     The following logarithms are all to the same base:

                                          log(ab2 c) = 7
                                         log(a2 bc2 ) = 11
                                        log(a2 b2 c3 ) = 15


     What is this base?

     A a

     B b

     C c

     D It is possible to determine the base, but the base is not a, b or c.

     E There is insuﬃcient information given to determine the base.




                                              17

stem.rawText:
The numbers a, b and c are each greater than 1.

     The following logarithms are all to the same base:

                                          log(ab2 c) = 7
                                         log(a2 bc2 ) = 11
                                        log(a2 b2 c3 ) = 15


     What is this base?

options.label: D
options.rawText: It is possible to determine the base, but the base is not a, b or c.

options.label: E
options.rawText: There is insuﬃcient information given to determine the base.

solution.rawText:
We expand the expressions using the logarithm rules, namely log(ab) = log a + log b and log an =
n log a when a > 1. This gives

                                    log a + 2 log b + log c = 7
                                  2 log a + log b + 2 log c = 11
                                  2 log a + 2 log b + 3 log c = 15

This is a set of simultaneous equations! If we write A = log a, B = log b and C = log c, they
become

                                        A + 2B + C = 7                                         (1)
                                       2A + B + 2C = 11                                        (2)
                                       2A + 2B + 3C = 15                                       (3)

Subtracting (3) − (2) and (3) − 2 × (1) to eliminate A gives

                                             B+C =4                                            (4)
                                          −2B + C = 1                                          (5)

Subtracting these then gives
                                             3B = 3
so B = 1 (and thus C = 3, A = 2).
Therefore log b = 1, so the base of logarithms is b (and also a = b2 , c = b3 ). The correct answer
is option B.




c UCLES                                                                                    Page 19
